LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS HORNBLOWER YACHTS, INC., TRACY SALVADOR, LEESA SINGER AND LARISA THOMAS' PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO DISMISS OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, STAY THE PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. THE COURT STAYS THIS ACTION. PLAINTIFFS AGREED TO ARBITRATE THE INSTANT DISPUTE BY EXECUTING THE ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF POLICIES AND ACKNOWLEDGEMENT & AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING A WAIVER. DEFENDANTS MOVED TO ARBITRATE ALMOST IMMEDIATELY AFTER THEY WERE SERVED WITH THE COMPLAINT. THERE IS NO AUTHORITY PROVIDING THAT A DEFENDANT NEEDS TO SEEK ARBITRATION PRIOR TO THE COMMENCEMENT OF LITIGATION. DEFENDANT'S AGENTS HAVE THE RIGHT TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. SEE DRYER V. LA RAMS, (1985) 40 CAL.3D 406, 418. EVEN ASSUMING THAT PLAINTIFFS DEMONSTRATE THAT THE CONTRACTS ARE PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE, THEY FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY BECAUSE THE AGREEMENTS ARE MUTUAL AND PRESERVE ALL RIGHTS AND REMEDIES THE PARTIES WOULD HAVE HAD IN COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arb Adn To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay The Proceedings
Off Calendar May-03-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: APRIL L. WEAVER, ESQ., 415.591.1501, FOR DEFENDANTS HORNBLOWER YACHTS, INC., TRACY SALVADOR, LEESA SINGER AND LARISA THOMAS; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs HORNBLOWER YACHTS, INC., TRACY SALVADOR, LEESA SINGER and LARISA THOMAS' Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Stay The Proceedings is granted. The Court stays this action. Plaintiffs agreed to arbitrate the instant dispute by executing the ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF POLICIES and ACKNOWLEDGEMENT & AGREEMENT. Plaintiffs have not met their burden of demonstrating a waiver. Defendants moved to arbitrate almost immediately after they were served with the complaint. There is no authority providing that a defendant needs to seek arbitration prior to the commencement of litigation. Defendant's agents have the right to compel arbitration. See Dryer v. LA Rams, (1985) 40 Cal.3d 406, 418. Even assuming that plaintiffs demonstrate that the contracts are procedurally unconscionable, they fail to demonstrate substantive unconscionability because the agreements are mutual and preserve all rights and remedies the parties would have had in court. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
